title: Jedi Quest: The False Peace
content:
{{Top|rwm|imp|italics=1}} {{Book |image=[[File:The_False_Peace.jpg]] |title=''Jedi Quest: The False Peace'' |author=[[Jude Watson]] |cover artist=*[[Alicia Buelow]] *[[David Mattingly]] |illustrator= |editor= |publisher=[[Scholastic]] |early release= |release date=[[July]] [[2004]]''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' |intl release= |rereleased= |media type=Paperback |pages=158 |isbn=0439339251 |isbn2= |isbn3= |timeline=[[24 BBY/Legends|24 BBY]] |series=[[Star Wars: Jedi Quest (novel series)|''Star Wars: Jedi Quest'']] |preceded by=[[Jedi Quest: The Changing of the Guard|''The Changing of the Guard'']] |followed by=[[Jedi Quest: The Final Showdown|''The Final Showdown'']] }} '''''Jedi Quest: The False Peace''''' is the ninth in a [[Star Wars: Jedi Quest (novel series)|series]] of young reader novels that chronicle the adventures of [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] between [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']] and [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']]. Written by [[Jude Watson]], this book features an [[Attack on the Senate Building|attempt]] by [[Granta Omega]] and [[Jenna Zan Arbor]], enemies of the Jedi, to destroy the entire [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Senate]]. Along with [[Siri Tachi/Legends|Siri Tachi]], a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]], and [[Ferus Olin]], her [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] must stop the two criminals and bring safety to the [[Senate Building/Legends|Senate walls]] again. ==Publisher's summary==
'''Obi-Wan Kenobi and Anakin Skywalker.'''
'''Master and apprentice.'''
'''Chosen by fate. Destined for conflict.'''
''The Senate makes the laws that ensure peace and justice in the galaxy. It is meant to keep chaos and lawlessness from taking over.'' ''But what if the Senators are not safe? What if they are about to be attacked by powerful and sinister forces?'' ''Under the watchful eye of Supreme Chancellor Palpatine, Anakin Skywalker and Obi-Wan Kenobi must counter the threat to the Senate—or the whole galaxy might be thrown into a violent darkness.'' ==Plot summary== The [[Hunting/Legends|hunt]] for [[Jenna Zan Arbor]] and [[Granta Omega]] is cut short when the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] are recalled to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] by [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. A rising tide of [[Anti-Jedi sentiment/Legends|anti-Jedi sentiment]] risks [[Galactic Senate/Legends|senatorial]] support for the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Order]]. Leading this [[Politics/Legends|political]] movement is [[Bog Divinian]], shortsighted servant of [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Sano Sauro]]. The two conspire to replace Palpatine in a coup with the secret assistance of Zan Arbor and Omega. Divinian’s wife, [[Astri Oddo]], gives [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] the information he needs to uncover the impending attack on the Senate. Obi-Wan, [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], [[Siri Tachi/Legends|Siri Tachi]] and [[Ferus Olin]] are central to deflecting the assault of deadly [[Seeker (droid)/Legends|seeker droids]], though once again Zan Arbor and Omega escape. Palpatine neutralizes Sauro’s ambitions by promoting him to [[Deputy Chancellor]], thus keeping his enemy close. Anakin learns much from watching Palpatine’s decisiveness in action, and he admires the man’s convictions. The Jedi help relocate Astri and her son, [[Lune Oddo Divinian|Lune]], away from Bog. ==Continuity== Though set between [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|Episodes I]] and [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|II]], the novel was written as [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|Episode III]] was in development. [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin’s]] relationship was portrayed in a manner that better reflected their camaraderie in ''Revenge of the Sith'' ([[2005]]). [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan’s]] [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Senatorial]] informant, [[Tyro Caladian]], was [[Death/Legends|killed]] in the course of the story for uncovering a shocking truth about the Senate. Though it wasn’t explicitly stated, Tyro was presumably silenced for learning about the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] presence on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. ==Media== {{Mediacat|from=1|imagecat=Images from Jedi Quest: The False Peace}} ===Editions=== *{{ISBN|0439339251}}; [[July]] [[2004]], [[Scholastic]], 158-page paperback *{{ISBN|1417637323}}; July 2004, [[Tandem Library Books]], 158-page
